message:
Storage depth accounting fix, input trimming, and reservation field correction

- storage/lib.rs: Fix CommitCoalescer depth accounting to be precise
  rather than approximate. Move depth.fetch_add to happen at the exact
  enqueue point (including the optimistic fast path), and depth.fetch_sub
  to happen right after draining the batch and after draining spill.
  Remove the racy fetch_update-based saturating subtract and the
  depth.store(0) reset that could mask a concurrent enqueue.

- contacts.rs: Trim whitespace from from_agent before resolving or
  registering sender, preventing "agent " and "agent" from creating
  duplicate entries.

- messaging.rs: Trim whitespace from recipient names before lookup,
  matching the contacts.rs fix for consistency.

- reservations.rs: Fix JSON output in file_reservation_paths,
  release_file_reservations, and renew_file_reservations to use
  agent.name instead of the local agent_name variable, which could
  be stale after agent re-resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -472,7 +472,7 @@ pub async fn file_reservation_paths(
             .map(|r| {
                 serde_json::json!({
                     "id": r.id.unwrap_or(0),
-                    "agent": &agent_name,
+                    "agent": &agent.name,
                     "path_pattern": &r.path_pattern,
                     "exclusive": r.exclusive != 0,
                     "reason": &r.reason,
@@ -576,7 +576,7 @@ pub async fn release_file_reservations(
             .map(|r| {
                 serde_json::json!({
                     "id": r.id.unwrap_or(0),
-                    "agent": &agent_name,
+                    "agent": &agent.name,
                     "path_pattern": &r.path_pattern,
                     "exclusive": r.exclusive != 0,
                     "reason": &r.reason,
@@ -696,7 +696,7 @@ pub async fn renew_file_reservations(
             .map(|r| {
                 serde_json::json!({
                     "id": r.id.unwrap_or(0),
-                    "agent": &agent_name,
+                    "agent": &agent.name,
                     "path_pattern": &r.path_pattern,
                     "exclusive": r.exclusive != 0,
                     "reason": &r.reason,
